page: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute server configured with 24 shared vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz, containing 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. The CPU supports AVX and AVX-512 instruction sets, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2 and FMA extensions. Local storage is bundled with 1600 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations place the server's performance consistently in the average tier across most workloads. The stress-ng single-core score of 1451.3 and multi-core score of 32078.94 reflect standard multi-threaded capabilities, supported by a PassMark CPU score of 29089.76. Memory bandwidth is measured at 2192.16 GB/sec for cached small blocks, dropping to 114.95 GB/sec for read and 55.93 GB/sec for write operations on large uncached blocks. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in single-threaded gzip compression, which registers in the bottom 25% tier at 23.37 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs in the average range at 98.25 GB/sec.

With its shared CPU allocation, the vc2-24c-96gb offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require high resource density and substantial local storage without requiring dedicated CPU cores. Database operations are supported by a PassMark database score of 6984.96 and Redis SET performance of 31.68 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput reaches 15.77 GB/sec. Additionally, the instance can handle light machine learning tasks, achieving 33.17 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 9.96 tokens/sec for text generation on a medium 7B LLM model. This server is best suited for general-purpose web serving, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing where shared CPU resources are acceptable.
description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute instance featuring 24 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs (12 physical cores) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 96.0 GB of RAM and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with a PassMark CPU score of 29089.76 and a Redis SET throughput of 31.68 million operations per second. However, single-threaded gzip compression is a relative bottleneck, performing in the bottom 25% tier. The instance provides a cost-efficient, high-density resource profile for workloads that tolerate shared CPU allocation. It is well-suited for general-purpose web serving, database operations, and moderate multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Vultr vc2-24c-96gb is a Cloud Compute server configured with 24 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96 GB of RAM, and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.

tagline: A high-capacity shared-CPU instance combining 96 GB of memory and 1.6 TB NVMe storage for balanced, data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 24 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs and 96.0 GB RAM on x86_64 architecture
1600 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Average multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 29089.76
Weak single-threaded gzip compression performance of 23.37 GB/sec
Suitable for database hosting, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads
